cards.back: Z = R + j(ωL − 1/ωC). Real part R, imaginary part is net reactance X = XL − XC.
ωL = 1/ωC → net reactance = 0 → Z = R. Reactances cancel each other.
ω₀ = 1/√LC rad/s, f₀ = 1/(2π√LC) Hz. Depends only on L and C, not R.
Z = R (min), I = Vm/R (max), φ = 0°, PF = 1, LC = short circuit.
|VL| = |VC| = Q × Vs. Can be many times the source voltage. VL and VC are 180° apart (cancel in KVL).
Capacitive. XC > XL → net reactance negative → impedance phase negative → current leads voltage.
Inductive. XL > XC → net reactance positive → impedance phase positive → current lags voltage.
VR = Vs (horizontal). VL points up, VC points down, both magnitude Q×Vs. VL + VC = 0.
Component voltages (VL, VC) can far exceed source voltage. A 10V source with Q=25 creates 250V across L and C.
V-shaped curve: high at low ω (capacitive), minimum R at ω₀, high again at high ω (inductive).
